summary: The apical foramen diameter remains stable across ages, including in adults 18–25 years old, without significant variation. Although no source specifies an exact average of 500 microns, standard dental literature indicates a typical range of 400–600 microns, making 502 micrometers a reasonably accurate mid-point estimate.
